facts.text: The modern Summer Olympics were first held in Athens in 1896.
The Summer Games are staged every four years, except during major disruptions like World War I and World War II.
Track and field, swimming, gymnastics, and basketball are among the most watched Summer Olympic sports.
The Olympic motto was updated to 'Faster, Higher, Stronger — Together' in 2021.
The five Olympic rings symbolize the union of the world's inhabited continents.
The Summer Olympics have featured legendary athletes such as Jesse Owens, Carl Lewis, Usain Bolt, Simone Biles, and Michael Phelps.
Michael Phelps became the most decorated Olympian in history through his swimming success at multiple Summer Games.
Usain Bolt's sprint performances at Beijing, London, and Rio made him one of the defining Summer Olympians of the 21st century.
The 1936 Berlin Olympics became one of the most politically charged Games in history.
The 1968 Mexico City Olympics are remembered for both world records and the iconic Black Power salute by Tommie Smith and John Carlos.
Women have participated in the modern Olympics since 1900, and their share of events has grown dramatically over time.
New sports such as skateboarding, sport climbing, and surfing were added to recent Summer Olympics to appeal to younger audiences.
The Summer Olympics returned to Paris in 2024, exactly a century after the city last hosted in 1924.
Host cities often use the Summer Olympics to build transportation, venues, and global visibility — though the cost can be enormous.
Olympic gold medals are mostly silver with a gold plating rather than being made of solid gold.
The Summer Olympics are one of the largest recurring sporting events on Earth.
The Summer Olympic Games became one of the largest and most watched sporting events in the world.
Its global scale helped make it a defining part of international athletics.
